rag_rules: Use only visible AADL features or eq helpers declared in the same annex for guarantees
Move implementation-level bindings to the implementation annex or delete invalid guarantees
Use AGREE scalar types: int, real, or bool for const and eq declarations
Use assign only when the left-hand side is an existing output feature or declared auxiliary variable in implementation scope
generic_rules: Trace the first unresolved identifier or invalid helper declaration on the same line for type errors
Use only visible ports for type-level guarantees
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers, data types, or property references absent from the analyzed model
Use only valid implementation bindings in the implementation annex
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Component implementation annexes are for implementation-level bindings
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex
Do not use AGREE scalar type names like 'integer' or 'boolean'
Do not use assign in type scope
